items.title: Labor, Greens cut deal on CGT and negative gearing

children.content: The Greens pledge their support in exchange for an end to an exemption that allows self-managed super funds to borrow money to invest in residential property, and a delay on the overhaul of the NDIS.

articles.title: Hanson says Ben Roberts-Smith absence from Anzac Hall opening 'disappointing'

children.content: Victoria Cross recipient and alleged war criminal Ben Roberts-Smith will no longer attend an event at the Australian War Memorial tonight. Follow live.

image.altText: A man wearing many medals stands out from the crowd.

articles.title: Angus Taylor refuses to commit to a multicultural Australia

children.content: The opposition leader, pressed multiple times on whether his party still supported multiculturalism or endorsed One Nation leader Pauline Hanson's vision of a "monoculture", refused to commit to either.

image.altText: A man speaks.

articles.title: Pauline Hanson clarifies she will not seek to cut paid parental leave

children.content: Pauline Hanson has clarified she would not seek to cut government-funded paid parental leave if One Nation were in power after opening the door to a wind-back at her National Press Club address last week.

image.altText: A woman in a red, with red hair, standing in a corridor in front of TV cameras.

items.title: Dead birds to be tested for H5N1 bird flu after washing up on SA coast

children.content: Two dead seabirds have been collected and are being tested for the H5N1 bird flu virus in South Australia, as well as another two wild birds in Western Australia, following Australia's first confirmed cases of the deadly variant this week.

image.altText: A hand with a cloth holding the beak of a dead grey and white bird on a beach.

items.title: Senior Albanese minister clashes with Pocock over top-secret AI copyright claim

children.content: Federal politician David Pocock has clashed with a senior Albanese government minister over a whistleblower claim that federal cabinet is considering plans to let AI companies train on Australian copyright material.

image.altText: Pocock gestures with both hands, partially obscured by the silhouettes of press gallery journalists he is speaking to.

items.title: Court told alleged cult leader groomed girl to have 45 of his children

children.content: William "Little Pebble" Kamm has denied allegations he groomed a girl for more than a decade after he was accused of declaring in phone calls that it was her "mission" to become his queen.

image.altText: An old man with grey hair, bushy eyebrows and glasses walking towards a building.

items.title: Kids Helpline crisis interventions on the rise again towards COVID high

children.content: Kids Helpline says a growing number of Australian young people are requiring immediate emergency help, with a concerning trend around crisis interventions.

image.altText: Girl sitting down with her hands clasped.

items.title: 'We lost our son': India's medical exams scandal leads to tragedy and uproar

children.content: More than 2 million students across India have resat the country's highly competitive medical entrance exam after last month's test was cancelled amid allegations that questions had been leaked.

items.title: 'They've been caught out': KPMG fallout continues with resignations

items.title: Video of nurse allegedly threatening Israelis excluded from court case

children.content: Lawyers argued the viral video clip from an online chat with an Israeli influencer breached NSW laws prohibiting recording private conversations without consent.

items.title: Alessandro Circati chose Australia over Italy. His dad approves

children.content: Alessandro Circati's decision to play for the Socceroos raised eyebrows within his family in Italy, but his father says they now realise that, deep down, he's not Italian.

items.title: Can Australia still make the knockout rounds of the World Cup?

children.content: The FIFA World Cup is well underway and it's starting to become clear which teams are progressing through to the next round of competition. But, Australia's fate still isn't sealed.
Australia currently sitting second in its group with one win against Türkiye and one loss against the United States.
We break down how the new qualifying process works, what's changed, and what the Socceroos need from their match on Friday against Paraguay.
